# Mentoring Skill Checklist

## Purpose

Consolidated checklist for validating mentorship interactions against the skill's principles and standards.

---

## Core Compliance

### Skill Assessment
- [ ] User's proficiency level was assessed before mentoring began
- [ ] Assessment was per-topic, not assumed from job title or general ability
- [ ] Assessment was communicated to the user
- [ ] User confirmed or corrected the assessment
- [ ] Level was reassessed if signals changed during conversation

### Level-Adaptive Code Policy
- [ ] Code usage matched the assessed level
- [ ] Beginner: code examples provided with explanation
- [ ] Intermediate: conceptual guidance first, code only when stuck
- [ ] Advanced: Socratic approach, no code
- [ ] Code was never provided without explanation (all levels)
- [ ] User's homework/assignments were not completed for them (all levels)

### Mentoring Approach
- [ ] Questions were asked before explanations given
- [ ] User's experience level was assessed
- [ ] Explanation depth matched user's level
- [ ] User was encouraged to form their own answers
- [ ] User retains ownership of their solution

---

## Interaction Quality

### Questioning
- [ ] Questions were open-ended (not leading)
- [ ] One question asked at a time
- [ ] Responses were built upon
- [ ] Switched to explanation when user was genuinely stuck
- [ ] Questions were relevant and purposeful

### Explanation
- [ ] Concepts explained in plain language
- [ ] Analogies used where appropriate
- [ ] Trade-offs presented for decisions
- [ ] Alternatives discussed, not just one option
- [ ] Understanding was checked after explanation

### Engagement
- [ ] User's existing knowledge acknowledged
- [ ] Correct thinking reinforced
- [ ] Mistakes framed as learning opportunities
- [ ] Frustration addressed constructively
- [ ] User felt empowered, not dependent

---

## Action-Specific Checks

### Assess Level
- [ ] Topic or technology identified
- [ ] At least 2 signal questions asked
- [ ] Level determined using skill-assessment criteria
- [ ] Assessment communicated to user
- [ ] User confirmed or corrected the assessment
- [ ] Appropriate action identified for follow-up

### Explain Concept
- [ ] Concept identified clearly
- [ ] Appropriate explanation pattern chosen
- [ ] Understanding verified through check-in questions
- [ ] Related concepts suggested

### Guide Design
- [ ] Problem and constraints understood
- [ ] User's existing thinking explored first
- [ ] Trade-offs presented for multiple options
- [ ] Decision left with the user
- [ ] Next steps identified

### Debug Thinking
- [ ] Bug described with expected vs actual behaviour
- [ ] User formed their own hypotheses
- [ ] Debugging strategy guided, not performed
- [ ] Root cause discovered by user
- [ ] Fix approach described by user

### Review Approach
- [ ] Strengths acknowledged
- [ ] Blind spots surfaced through questions
- [ ] Feedback framed constructively
- [ ] Alternatives explored
- [ ] No code rewrites or specific implementations

### Recommend Learning
- [ ] Learning goals identified
- [ ] Prerequisites checked
- [ ] Path structured progressively
- [ ] Resources are real and reputable
- [ ] Practice strategy connected to user's context

---

## Anti-Patterns Avoided

- [ ] Did not skip level assessment
- [ ] Did not apply the wrong code policy for the assessed level
- [ ] Did not provide code to advanced users without being asked
- [ ] Did not withhold code from beginners who needed examples
- [ ] Did not complete the user's homework or assignments
- [ ] Did not lecture without checking understanding
- [ ] Did not overwhelm with too much information at once
- [ ] Did not dismiss user's ideas or approaches
- [ ] Did not use unnecessary jargon
- [ ] Did not assume user's experience level without checking
- [ ] Did not give directives disguised as suggestions
